comment: Constant-curvature Riemannian model spaces. Euclidean E^n (K=0), spherical S^n (K=+1), hyperbolic H^n (K=-1). Metrics from NIST DLMF §1.6 (vectors and tensors), Bransden-Joachain (2003) for the central-potential context, and standard differential-geometry conventions. The line-element forms shown use unit curvature radius.

primitives.name: Euclidean line E^1
primitives.notes: Trivial flat 1-manifold. Isometry group is ℝ ⋊ ℤ₂ (translations and reflection).

primitives.name: Euclidean 3-space E^3
primitives.notes: Default ambient space for non-relativistic physics. Isometry group E(3) = ℝ³ ⋊ O(3).

primitives.name: circle S^1
primitives.notes: Isometry group O(2). Universal cover of itself is ℝ via the exponential map.

primitives.name: sphere S^2
primitives.notes: Isometry group O(3). Eigenfunctions of the Laplace-Beltrami operator are spherical harmonics Y_l^m.

primitives.name: 3-sphere S^3
primitives.notes: S³ is the universal (double) cover of SO(3). As a Lie group, S³ ≅ SU(2) ≅ Spin(3). The hidden SO(4) symmetry of the hydrogen Coulomb problem realises S³ as the bound-state momentum manifold.

primitives.name: hyperbolic line H^1
primitives.notes: In one dimension constant-curvature is degenerate; H^1 is isometric to E^1. Retained for completeness.

primitives.name: hyperbolic plane H^2
primitives.notes: Isometry group PSL(2,ℝ). Three standard models: Poincaré disk, upper half-plane, two-sheet hyperboloid in ℝ^{2,1}.

primitives.name: hyperbolic 3-space H^3

attrs.model: Poincaré ball, upper half-space, two-sheet hyperboloid in ℝ^{3,1}

primitives.notes: Isometry group PSL(2,ℂ). The momentum-space manifold of the H-atom continuum (E>0) is H³.
